portfolio: Reliability, panic discipline, crash recovery, partial-state handling
primary_outputs: Embedded findings in all four ADRs
notes: Position paper filed at docs/adr/positions/council-surgeon.md (1000 words). Top three reliability risks to press into ADRs: (1) SIGINT or power-loss data loss around the indexer — requires per-batch transactions, a scan_generation guard, WAL+integrity_check on open, and rename-not-repair on corruption; (2) panics in TUI-embedded code that leave the terminal raw or hang the UI on a dead worker — requires panic-only-on-programmer-error discipline, a global hook that disables raw mode, and join-handle monitors that respawn search workers; (3) stale filesystem state (deleted paths, TOCTOU between walk and action) — requires tombstones on action-time stat failure, never silent execvp against ghost paths. Awaiting Wave 2 ADR drafts for review input.
